name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (pyruvate → acetyl-CoA)
summary: The mitochondrial pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (PDC) irreversibly oxidizes pyruvate to acetyl-CoA, releasing CO2 and reducing NAD+, linking glycolysis to the citric acid cycle.
description: The pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (PDC) is a giant multienzyme machine in the mitochondrial matrix that catalyzes the oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate to acetyl-CoA. It is the committed, physiologically irreversible gateway that commits carbohydrate-derived carbon to oxidation in the TCA cycle (or, via citrate export, to fatty-acid synthesis). Three catalytic components act in sequence on five cofactors (thiamine diphosphate, lipoamide, coenzyme A, FAD, NAD+): E1 (pyruvate dehydrogenase, EC 1.2.4.1; a PDHA1/PDHB α2β2 heterotetramer) decarboxylates pyruvate on a TPP carbanion and reductively acetylates the lipoyl arm of E2; E2 (dihydrolipoyllysine-residue acetyltransferase, EC 2.3.1.12; DLAT) transfers the acetyl group from S-acetyldihydrolipoamide to CoA, giving acetyl-CoA; E3 (dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase, EC 1.8.1.4; DLD) reoxidizes the resulting dihydrolipoamide, passing electrons through its FAD to NAD+ to form NADH. E2 and the E3-binding protein (PDHX/protein X) build the pentagonal-dodecahedral inner core that tethers ~20-30 E1 heterotetramers and ~6-12 E3 homodimers (Patel et al., 2014). Activity is governed by a reversible phosphorylation switch: four pyruvate dehydrogenase kinases (PDK1-4) phosphorylate E1α to inactivate the complex, while two Ca2+/Mg2+-dependent phosphatases (PDP1-2) dephosphorylate and reactivate it. The kinases are tuned by the acetyl-CoA/CoA and NADH/NAD+ ratios (activating) and by pyruvate, ADP and CoA (inhibiting), so PDC flux tracks cellular energy and redox state. PDK4 is transcriptionally induced during fasting and diabetes (PPARα, glucocorticoid, FOXO signaling), sparing pyruvate for gluconeogenesis. Inherited PDC deficiency (most commonly PDHA1 mutations) causes lactic acidosis and neurological disease.

references.title: The pyruvate dehydrogenase complexes: structure-based function and regulation
Regulation of pyruvate metabolism and human disease

compartments.name: Mitochondrial matrix
compartments.description: Soluble mitochondrial compartment where the PDC and TCA-cycle enzymes reside; pyruvate is delivered here from the cytosol by the mitochondrial pyruvate carrier.

metabolites.name: Thiamine diphosphate (TPP)
metabolites.description: Prosthetic group of E1; the thiazolium C2 carbanion attacks the pyruvate carbonyl to drive decarboxylation.

metabolites.name: 2-(alpha-Hydroxyethyl)thiamine diphosphate
metabolites.description: The E1-bound enamine/carbanion intermediate carrying the two-carbon unit after decarboxylation.

metabolites.name: Enzyme N6-(lipoyl)lysine (oxidized lipoamide)
metabolites.description: The oxidized (cyclic disulfide) lipoyllysine swinging arm covalently attached to E2 (and PDHX).

metabolites.name: Enzyme N6-(S-acetyldihydrolipoyl)lysine
metabolites.description: The acetyl group is carried as a thioester on one sulfur of the reduced lipoyl arm before transfer to CoA.

metabolites.name: Enzyme N6-(dihydrolipoyl)lysine (reduced lipoamide)
metabolites.description: The reduced (dithiol) lipoyl arm left after the acetyl group is handed to CoA; reoxidized by E3.

metabolites.name: FAD
metabolites.description: Prosthetic group of E3; accepts electrons from dihydrolipoamide (FAD→FADH2) and passes them to NAD+.

metabolites.name: Insulin
metabolites.description: Anabolic hormone that promotes PDC dephosphorylation/activation, chiefly in adipose tissue and liver.

enzymes.name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase E1 component subunit alpha (somatic)
enzymes.description: Catalytic α subunit of E1; TPP- and Mg2+-dependent decarboxylation of pyruvate. Its regulatory serines (Ser293/Ser300/Ser232) are the phosphorylation on/off switch of the whole complex.

enzymes.name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase E1 component subunit beta
enzymes.description: β subunit completing the E1 heterotetramer; helps form the TPP-binding active site.

enzymes.name: Dihydrolipoyllysine-residue acetyltransferase (E2)
enzymes.oligomerization: forms the pentagonal-dodecahedral inner core of the complex
enzymes.description: Transacetylase whose lipoyl domains form the swinging arm that shuttles the acetyl group; builds the structural core. Major autoantigen (M2) in primary biliary cholangitis.

enzymes.name: Dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase (E3)
enzymes.description: Shared E3 flavoenzyme (also used by the 2-oxoglutarate and branched-chain 2-oxo acid dehydrogenases and the glycine cleavage system); reoxidizes dihydrolipoamide and reduces NAD+.

enzymes.name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase protein X (E3-binding protein)
enzymes.oligomerization: integral to the E2 core; tethers E3
enzymes.description: Structural (non-catalytic) core component that anchors the E3 dimer to the E2 core; also called protein X.

enzymes.name: [Pyruvate dehydrogenase (acetyl-transferring)] kinase isozyme 1
enzymes.description: PDH kinase isoform; phosphorylates E1α to inactivate PDC. Notably active on all three E1α phosphosites; upregulated by HIF-1α in hypoxia/cancer.

enzymes.name: [Pyruvate dehydrogenase (acetyl-transferring)] kinase isozyme 2
enzymes.description: Most widely expressed PDH kinase; the isoform most sensitive to acute regulation by pyruvate, ADP, NADH and acetyl-CoA.

enzymes.name: [Pyruvate dehydrogenase (acetyl-transferring)] kinase isozyme 3
enzymes.description: PDH kinase isoform with tissue-restricted expression; relatively insensitive to pyruvate inhibition.

enzymes.name: [Pyruvate dehydrogenase (acetyl-transferring)] kinase isozyme 4
enzymes.description: Fasting/diabetes-induced isoform (PPARα, glucocorticoid, FOXO targets); chronically suppresses glucose oxidation to spare carbon for gluconeogenesis.

enzymes.name: [Pyruvate dehydrogenase (acetyl-transferring)]-phosphatase 1
enzymes.description: Ca2+/Mg2+-dependent phosphatase that dephosphorylates E1α to reactivate PDC; the dominant isoform in heart and skeletal muscle, activated by Ca2+ during contraction.

enzymes.name: [Pyruvate dehydrogenase (acetyl-transferring)]-phosphatase 2
enzymes.description: Mg2+-dependent PDH phosphatase enriched in adipose tissue and liver; its activity is enhanced by insulin signaling.

complexes.name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (PDC)
complexes.description: ~9 MDa mammalian assembly: an E2/PDHX pentagonal-dodecahedral inner core (~60 subunits) that non-covalently tethers ~20-30 E1 (PDHA1/PDHB) heterotetramers and ~6-12 E3 homodimers, plus the regulatory PDK and PDP enzymes docked on the lipoyl domains.

reactions.name: Oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate to acetyl-CoA (net reaction)
reactions.equation: Pyruvate + CoA + NAD+ -> Acetyl-CoA + CO2 + NADH

provenance.notes: Net reaction of the three-component complex (EC 1.2.4.1 + 2.3.1.12 + 1.8.1.4; combined EC 1.2.1.104). Physiologically irreversible and the committed step of glucose oxidation.

reactions.name: E1: oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate (hydroxyethyl-TPP formation)
reactions.equation: Pyruvate + Thiamine diphosphate + H+ -> 2-(alpha-Hydroxyethyl)thiamine diphosphate + CO2

provenance.notes: Loss of CO2 makes this step effectively irreversible; it is the rate-limiting reaction of the complex. KEGG writes the isolated half-reaction as reversible.

reactions.name: E1: reductive acetylation of the lipoyl arm
reactions.equation: 2-(alpha-Hydroxyethyl)thiamine diphosphate + Enzyme N6-(lipoyl)lysine -> Enzyme N6-(S-acetyldihydrolipoyl)lysine + Thiamine diphosphate

provenance.notes: E1 transfers the two-carbon unit to the oxidized lipoyllysine of E2, regenerating TPP and reducing the disulfide.

reactions.name: E2: acetyl transfer to coenzyme A
reactions.equation: Enzyme N6-(S-acetyldihydrolipoyl)lysine + CoA -> Acetyl-CoA + Enzyme N6-(dihydrolipoyl)lysine

provenance.notes: KEGG entry R02569 is written in the acetyl-CoA-consuming (reverse) direction; shown here in the physiological pyruvate-oxidizing direction. Leaves the E2 lipoyl arm in the reduced dithiol form.

reactions.name: E3: reoxidation of dihydrolipoamide and NADH formation
reactions.equation: Enzyme N6-(dihydrolipoyl)lysine + NAD+ -> Enzyme N6-(lipoyl)lysine + NADH + H+

cofactors.role: prosthetic electron relay (FAD ⇌ FADH2 within E3)

provenance.notes: Net E3 reaction: electrons pass from the reduced lipoyl dithiol through enzyme-bound FAD (FAD→FADH2) to NAD+, regenerating the oxidized lipoyl arm and closing the catalytic cycle.

regulations.mechanism: PDK1 phosphorylates E1α (Ser293/site 1, and also sites 2/3), locking E1 in an inactive conformation and switching the complex off.
PDK2, the most widely expressed isoform, phosphorylates E1α serine sites to inactivate PDC; it is the main acutely metabolite-regulated kinase.
PDK3 phosphorylates E1α to inactivate PDC; expressed in a tissue-restricted pattern and relatively insensitive to pyruvate.
PDK4 phosphorylates and inactivates E1α; it is transcriptionally induced during fasting, starvation and diabetes (PPARα/glucocorticoid/FOXO), chronically suppressing glucose oxidation.
PDP1 (Ca2+/Mg2+-dependent) removes the inhibitory phosphates from E1α, reactivating the complex; dominant in heart and skeletal muscle.
PDP2 (Mg2+-dependent) dephosphorylates E1α to reactivate PDC; enriched in adipose and liver and stimulated by insulin signaling.
A high acetyl-CoA/CoA ratio stimulates PDK (via the E2 lipoyl domain), promoting E1α phosphorylation and shutdown of PDC — product feedback that reports the mitochondrion is acetyl-CoA replete.
A high NADH/NAD+ ratio stimulates PDK, promoting phosphorylation/inactivation of PDC when reducing equivalents are abundant.
Pyruvate inhibits PDK (most strongly PDK2), keeping PDC dephosphorylated and active when its substrate is plentiful.
ADP (a low-energy-charge signal) inhibits PDK, favoring the active, dephosphorylated complex when ATP demand is high.
Ca2+ binds and activates PDP1; in contracting muscle and heart, rising matrix Ca2+ dephosphorylates and activates PDC to match acetyl-CoA supply to ATP demand.
Acetyl-Coa product-inhibits the E2 transacetylase (reverse reaction / competition at the CoA site); a high acetyl-CoA/CoA ratio directly slows flux in addition to activating PDK.
NADH product-inhibits the E3 dehydrogenase by driving the terminal electron transfer backward; a high NADH/NAD+ ratio directly slows the complex.

Insulin promotes dephosphorylation/activation of PDC (via stimulation of PDP activity), most prominently in adipose tissue and liver, increasing pyruvate oxidation and supply of acetyl-CoA for lipogenesis.

provenance.notes: Insulin's activation of PDH is well documented physiologically (chiefly adipose/liver); the precise molecular coupling to PDP is less sharply defined, hence medium confidence.

relations.note: Pyruvate is the end product of glycolysis; it is carried into the matrix by the mitochondrial pyruvate carrier before oxidation by PDC.
Acetyl-CoA condenses with oxaloacetate (citrate synthase) to enter the TCA cycle — the principal fate of PDC-derived acetyl units.
NADH produced by E3 is reoxidized at Complex I of the electron transport chain, coupling PDC flux to ATP synthesis.
PDC is the main source of lipogenic acetyl-CoA, which is exported to the cytosol as citrate for de novo fatty-acid synthesis.
Acetyl-CoA is the substrate for hepatic ketogenesis when it exceeds TCA-cycle capacity.
Reciprocal control during fasting: acetyl-CoA allosterically activates pyruvate carboxylase, diverting pyruvate to oxaloacetate/gluconeogenesis while PDK4 keeps PDC switched off.
